uncertainties: The shipped template references {{company.name}} in the subject, HTML header/body, and text body, but the send-time call site (server/src/lib/eventBus/subscribers/creditExpiringSubscriber.ts, lines 131-143) assembles the data object with a `client: { id, name }` shape and NO `company` key. As written, {{company.name}} will render empty at send time; the actual client name is carried on `client.name`. The sample data in templateSampleData.ts (key 'credit-expiration') likewise supplies `client.name`, not `company.name`. This is a template/data path mismatch, not a dropped variable — flagging rather than silently reconciling.

The sample-data registry key is 'credit-expiration' whereas the DB template name (upsertEmailTemplate) and the send-time templateName are both 'credit-expiring'. Sample data is keyed by notification subtype slug, so this naming difference is expected but noted.

credits.items[].transactionId is populated by re-resolving credit_tracking.transaction_id (may be undefined if no matching credit_tracking row); credits.items[].description resolves the linked transaction description (defaults to 'N/A') but is not referenced by the template.

The shared layout wrapper (_shared/emailLayout.cjs) contains {{ticket.title}}, {{ticket.metaLine}}, {{currentYear}}, {{companyName}} in its docstring examples and default footerText, but this template overrides footerText with copy.footer and passes no headerMeta, so none of those tokens appear in the rendered credit-expiring output. headerTitle is passed as {{company.name}} (already captured).

sharedBlocks.name: branding
sharedBlocks.notes: Footer / layout identity block injected by the shared _shared/emailLayout.cjs wrapper and by every auth template. The wrapper's default footer is '(c) {{currentYear}} {{companyName}}', but MOST non-auth templates override footerText with their own copy (e.g. projects and billing ship a custom 'Powered by Alga PSA' footer), so currentYear/companyName are frequently present in sample data yet render nowhere. Canonical names are currentYear + companyName; every other spelling below is a variant of companyName.

variables.description: The current calendar year shown in the copyright footer.
variables.notes: Always new Date().getFullYear() at send time. Rendered only by the layout default footer; unused wherever a template overrides footerText.

variables.description: Name shown as the sending organization in the footer / header.
variables.notes: VARIANTS for the same slot: tenantClientName (auth email-verification, from tenants.client_name), clientName (auth password-reset footer, from the passed org), tenant_name (surveys, tenant.client_name||tenant.name, default 'Your Team'), company.name (invoices/billing MSP sender). These are NOT interchangeable across categories - see client block for the client-vs-MSP-company collision.

variables.description: Portal/product name shown in subject, header banner and footer.
variables.notes: auth-only (no-account-found, tenant-recovery). process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PLATFORM_NAME || 'Client Portal'. Substituted via a custom regex replaceVariables(), not the Handlebars/DatabaseTemplateProcessor path.

sharedBlocks.name: contact
sharedBlocks.notes: Support / help contact pair. Canonical names contactEmail + contactPhone (appointments). auth carries the same concept under different names and defaults them to the literal 'Not provided'. In several appointment templates contactEmail/contactPhone are assembled but not rendered (staff-facing new-appointment-request), so availability is call-site specific.

variables.description: Support email address the recipient can reach out to (often also set as Reply-To).
variables.notes: VARIANTS: supportEmail (auth password-reset, also replyTo), clientLocationEmail (auth portal-invitation, defaults 'Not provided', also replyTo). Assembled-but-unused in new-appointment-request (staff template).

variables.description: Support phone number, shown only when provided.
variables.notes: Optional; every appointment template guards it with {{#if contactPhone}}. VARIANT: clientLocationPhone (auth portal-invitation, defaults 'Not provided').

sharedBlocks.name: client
sharedBlocks.notes: The end-customer company the message concerns. This is the most naming-fragmented concept in the whole set: it appears as company.name, client.name, clientName, ticket.clientName, company_name and companyName across categories, AND collides head-on with the MSP-sender company (invoices uses company.name for the MSP's OWN name while billing uses company.name for the CLIENT). billing/credit-expiring ships an ACTIVE bug: the template reads {{company.name}} but the subscriber only supplies client.name, so the client name renders empty in production.

variables.description: Name of the end-customer company the email is about.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: clientName (tickets flatten to ticket.clientName; appointments/auth use bare clientName), company.name (billing credit-expiring template path - MISMATCHED, see notes; also invoice-email/invoice-generated where company.name is instead the MSP sender), company_name (surveys, ticket.client_name), invoice.clientName (invoices generated/overdue/received). Registry must distinguish 'client company' from 'MSP sending company' - same path shape, opposite meaning.

variables.description: Internal identifier of the client company.
variables.notes: Only billing assembles it (clients.client_id, feeds credits.url); not rendered directly.

sharedBlocks.name: recipient
sharedBlocks.notes: The individual person the email greets. Canonical recipientName + recipientEmail. Enormously variant-heavy: the greeting name is called recipientName (sla), requesterName (appointments), contactName (auth portal-invitation), userName (auth password-reset), contact_name (surveys), technicianName (appointment-assigned-technician, where the technician IS the recipient), recipient.name (invoices). Note recipientName is REFERENCED but never supplied in both project-task-assigned templates, so the greeting renders nameless.

variables.description: Display name of the person receiving the email, used in the greeting.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: requesterName (appointments, optional - greeting is generic if absent), contactName (auth), userName (auth password-reset), contact_name (surveys, default ''), recipient.name (invoices - billing contact full_name or client name), technicianName (appointment-assigned-technician greeting). NOT populated in project-task-assigned-primary/-additional (guarded by {{#if recipientName}}, renders empty). sla assembles it per-recipient only on the email channel, defaulting to 'there'/'Team Member'.

variables.description: Email address of the recipient (typically the send-to address).
variables.notes: VARIANTS: requesterEmail (appointments - used as send-to, not rendered in received/approved/declined; but IS rendered in the staff new-appointment-request), email (auth - rendered in password-reset, unused in email-verification), recipient.email (invoices, assembled-unused), technicianEmail (appointment-assigned-technician, required as send-to but unrendered). Availability flips per template - do not assume block-global.

sharedBlocks.name: assignee
sharedBlocks.notes: The agent/technician/team responsible for the work item. Tickets nest under ticket.assignedTo*, appointments flatten to technician*, sla uses assigneeName. Tickets additionally pre-compose display strings (assignedDetails) that duplicate name+email.

variables.description: Full name of the assigned agent/technician.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: ticket.assignedToName (tickets), technicianName (appointments), assigneeName (sla-escalation, CONCAT first/last, default 'Unassigned'). Ticket client-facing templates render name only (email hidden).

variables.description: Email address of the assigned agent/technician.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: ticket.assignedToEmail (default 'Not assigned'/'Not provided'), technicianEmail (appointments). Assembled-but-unused / intentionally hidden in all client-facing ticket templates.

variables.description: Phone number of the assigned technician, shown only when present.
variables.notes: Appointments only (technicianPhone, {{#if}} guarded). No ticket equivalent.

variables.description: Pre-composed 'Name (email)' string for plain-text bodies.
variables.notes: Tickets only (ticket.assignedDetails). A derived/denormalized field - registry should mark it computed-from name+email.

variables.description: Name of the team assigned (when a team rather than an individual is assigned).
variables.notes: tickets ticket.teamName (ticket-team-assigned) only. Present only when the assignment event carries changes.assigned_team_id.

sharedBlocks.name: ticket
sharedBlocks.notes: Rich work-item block. Tickets use a fully NESTED ticket.* namespace (10 templates share one baseTicketContext with only a subset rendered per template). sla FLATTENS the same concept to top-level camelCase (ticketNumber/ticketTitle/ticketUrl/priorityName). surveys FLATTENS to snake_case (ticket_number/ticket_subject/ticket_closed_at). Three incompatible conventions for one entity - the single biggest registry challenge. Also note ticket.id is misnamed: it holds the human-readable ticket_number, not a UUID. Several ticket.* paths (summary, closedAt, updatedAt, comment.authorName, comment.body) exist ONLY in Polish translations and are never assembled - they render empty for pl recipients.

variables.description: Human-readable ticket number shown on the 'Ticket #' badge.
variables.notes: MISNOMER: mapped from ticket_number/ticketNumber, not a UUID. VARIANTS: ticketNumber (sla, flat), ticket_number (surveys, snake_case, falls back to ticket_id).

variables.description: The ticket's subject/title line.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: ticketTitle (sla), ticket_subject (surveys, ticket.title default '').

variables.description: One-line ticket#/priority/status summary under the header.
variables.notes: Derived/denormalized. In ticket-auto-close-warning it collapses to just 'Ticket #<number>'. No sla/survey equivalent.

variables.description: Client/company the ticket belongs to.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: clientName (sla, flat). Overlaps the client block - same value, ticket-scoped path.

variables.description: Priority level name; drives the priority badge.
variables.notes: VARIANT/DUPLICATE: sla ships BOTH priority and priorityName holding the same value (priorityName is assembled-unused in sla-breach/warning).

variables.description: Hex color for the priority badge background.
variables.notes: Tickets only; default '#8A4DEA'. Raw style value injected into HTML.

variables.description: Current workflow status name.
variables.notes: Tickets. sla has no status; slaType ('Response'/'Resolution') is a different concept.

variables.description: Board/queue the ticket lives on.
variables.notes: Tickets only. Hidden in client-facing variants.

variables.description: Combined 'Category / subcategory' display string.
variables.notes: Derived. Raw category and subcategory are also assembled but always available-unused (template always uses categoryDetails).

variables.description: Ticket description body (raw HTML via triple-stache in en/pt; escaped in fr/es/de/nl/it).
variables.example: Toner is jammed and red error light is on.
variables.notes: RAW-HTML in some locales. pl translations swap in {{ticket.summary}} (never assembled -> empty). descriptionText/descriptionHtml duplicates are assembled-unused.

variables.description: Deep link to the ticket ('View Ticket' button).
variables.notes: MSP vs client-portal variant chosen per recipient for the SAME path. VARIANTS: ticketUrl (sla - RELATIVE path '/msp/tickets/<id>', {{#if}} guarded), survey_url (surveys, different semantics - survey response link).

variables.description: Requester email+phone joined for the HTML contact block.
variables.notes: Derived. requesterDetails (name+email+phone) is the plain-text twin. Raw requesterEmail/requesterPhone are assembled-unused across ticket templates.

variables.description: Pre-rendered HTML list of field changes (raw via triple-stache).
variables.notes: RAW-HTML. ticket-updated/-closed. pl swaps in {{ticket.summary}} (unassembled). Assembled-unused in ticket-closed.

variables.description: HTML of the resolution comment shown in the 'Resolution' box (raw).
variables.notes: RAW-HTML, ticket-closed only. May be empty if no is_resolution comment exists.

variables.description: Full name of who closed the ticket (or 'System').
variables.notes: ticket-closed. pl also references {{ticket.closedAt}} which is NEVER assembled -> empty.

variables.description: Formatted creation date/time (Intl date-time with short tz).
variables.notes: ticket-created. Pre-formatted string, not ISO. createdBy/createdDetails companions.

variables.description: Name(s) of who updated (comma-joined on accumulated path, 'System' if unknown).
variables.notes: ticket-updated. pl references unassembled {{ticket.updatedAt}} -> empty. updateCount present only on accumulated-batch path (assembled-unused).

sharedBlocks.name: invoice

Only invoice-email has a real wired send path; the other three are seeded-but-unsent, so their variable contracts are template-inferred, not call-site-verified. Currency and date fields are expected PRE-FORMATTED (formatCurrency/formatDate upstream).

sharedBlocks.name: appointment
sharedBlocks.notes: Flat (non-nested) block recurring across the 5 appointment templates. Two naming pairs for the same slot depending on lifecycle stage: requestedDate/requestedTime (pending) vs appointmentDate/appointmentTime (confirmed/assigned). referenceNumber has a DUAL FORMAT problem: the public route emits 'APT-<base36>-<random>' while the portal emits an 8-char uppercased id slice, for the SAME template variable.

variables.description: Name of the requested/confirmed service.
variables.notes: Present in all 5 templates; also drives subjects/headerTitle.

variables.description: Confirmed appointment date, pre-formatted.
variables.notes: VARIANT for pending stage: requestedDate. Both are pre-formatted display strings.

variables.description: Confirmed appointment time, pre-formatted.
variables.notes: VARIANT for pending stage: requestedTime (rendered with a parenthesized tz, e.g. '2:00 PM (EDT)'). Timezone label differs by call site.

variables.description: Appointment length in minutes ('minutes' appended by template).
variables.notes: Consistent across all 5 templates.

variables.description: Short reference code the customer can quote.
variables.notes: DUAL FORMAT for one variable: public route = generateReferenceNumber() 'APT-<base36 ts>-<random>'; portal = appointment_request_id.slice(0,8).toUpperCase() e.g. 'A1B2C3D4'. declined template (management-only) always uses the id-slice form.

variables.description: Microsoft Teams join link when the appointment is online.
variables.notes: Optional, {{#if}} guarded. Shared by approved + assigned-technician templates.

variables.description: ICS calendar download link.
variables.notes: Optional, {{#if}} guarded. approved + assigned-technician.

sharedBlocks.name: project
sharedBlocks.notes: Nested project.* block shared across the project-scoped templates (created/updated/closed/assigned/milestone). A large baseContext is assembled once and each template renders only ~5 of ~14 fields, so most fields are available-unused per template. Several templates (milestone-completed) have NO send handler - contract is template-inferred. Raw-HTML in project.changes. project.startDate is a raw DB value (not pre-formatted), unlike most date fields elsewhere.

variables.description: Name of the project.
variables.notes: Rendered by every project template; drives subjects.

variables.description: Project description (BlockNote flattened to text).
variables.example: Roll out new endpoint protection to all client sites.
variables.notes: descriptionText (duplicate) and descriptionHtml (HTML twin) are assembled-unused alongside it.

variables.description: Current status label (default 'Unknown').
variables.notes: used in updated/closed; available-unused in created.

variables.description: Link to open the project.
variables.notes: client-portal vs MSP variant per recipient - EXCEPT project-assigned which is always the internal /msp URL.

variables.description: Pre-rendered HTML list of field changes (raw via triple-stache).
variables.notes: RAW-HTML. updated/closed only.

variables.description: Project start date.
variables.notes: RAW DB value (projects.start_date), NOT pre-formatted - inconsistent with pre-formatted date fields in other blocks.

variables.description: Project manager name (default 'Unassigned').
variables.notes: used in created; available-unused in updated/closed.

variables.description: Name of who updated/closed/assigned the project.
variables.notes: VARIANTS: closedBy (resolved name), assignedBy (default 'Someone'), createdBy (RAW user ID, NOT a display name - inconsistent resolution vs updatedBy/closedBy).

variables.description: Overall completion percentage (rendered with trailing %).
variables.notes: milestone-completed only (which has no send handler).

variables.description: Human-readable project number (projects.project_number).
variables.notes: Misnomer like ticket.id - it's a number, not a UUID. Assembled but unrendered in every project template.

variables.description: Name of the client the project belongs to (default 'No Client').
variables.notes: Assembled but never rendered by any project template. Overlaps the client block.

sharedBlocks.name: task
sharedBlocks.notes: Nested task.* block shared by the task templates (assigned-primary, assigned-additional, comment-added, updated). assigned-primary/-additional are byte-identical except task.role ('Primary Assignee' vs 'Additional Agent') and the greeting subject. task.description and recipientName are {{#if}}-referenced but NEVER supplied at send time. task-updated has no send handler. comment.* is a sub-block only used by task/ticket comment templates.

variables.description: Name of the task.
variables.notes: Drives subjects. Present in all task templates.

variables.description: Name of the project the task belongs to.
variables.notes: VARIANT: task-comment-added and task-updated use nested project.name instead of task.project for the same value - inconsistent within the same category.

variables.description: Task due date (row shown only when present).
variables.notes: {{#if}} guarded. Raw project_tasks.due_date.

variables.description: Name of who assigned the task (default 'Someone').
variables.notes: From event payload assignedByName.

variables.description: Recipient's role on the task.
variables.notes: 'Primary Assignee' vs 'Additional Agent' - the only meaningful data difference between the two assignment templates.

variables.description: MSP link to open the task within the project view.
variables.notes: Always MSP (no client-portal variant for tasks).

variables.description: Current task status label.
variables.notes: task-updated only (no send handler).

variables.description: Task completion percentage (trailing %).
variables.notes: task-updated only.

variables.description: Task description shown in a highlighted box when present.
variables.example: Apply the standard firewall baseline to all edge devices.
variables.notes: {{#if task.description}} REFERENCED but NEVER supplied by the send context in both assignment templates -> always renders empty. Registry must not treat as reliably populated.

observations: THREE incompatible naming conventions coexist for the same entity: nested dotted (ticket.*, invoice.*, project.*, task.*, company.name, credits.*), flat camelCase (appointment fields, sla ticketNumber/ticketTitle/slaType, auth verificationUrl/resetLink, opportunities digest.*), and flat snake_case (surveys ticket_number/contact_name/rating_buttons_html/tenant_name). The ticket concept alone spans all three: ticket.id (tickets), ticketNumber (sla), ticket_number (surveys).
ticket.id and project.id are misnamed: both hold a human-readable number (ticket_number / project_number), not a UUID. A registry that documents them as 'id' will mislead template authors.

Client-company vs MSP-sender company collide on the SAME path shape company.name: invoices/invoice-email uses company.name for the MSP's OWN name (the sender), while billing/credit-expiring uses company.name for the CLIENT. The registry needs two distinct canonical entries, not one 'company' block.

ACTIVE PRODUCTION BUG already in the corpus: billing/credit-expiring template reads {{company.name}} but the subscriber assembles client.name (no company key), so the client name renders empty. A naive registry codifying company.name would bless the broken path.

Localization variance: Polish (pl) translations reference paths that no call site assembles - ticket.summary (created/assigned/team-assigned/updated), ticket.closedAt (closed), ticket.updatedAt (updated), comment.authorName + comment.body (comment-added). French surveys reference the misspelled {{technicien_name}} vs the supplied technician_name. These render empty and are locale-conditional, so a block cannot promise a value 'available' across all locales.

Availability is call-site-specific, not block-global. The same variable is 'used' in one template and 'available-unused' in another (e.g. requesterEmail rendered in new-appointment-request but not in received/approved/declined; ticket.assignedToEmail rendered in MSP templates but intentionally hidden in client-facing ones; recipient.email/recipientName supplied-vs-referenced flips per template).

Heavy field duplication within blocks: sla ships priority AND priorityName (same value), and timeRemaining/remainingTime/timeOverdue (all the same formatRemainingTime output) - each template renders one and leaves the rest assembled-unused. Tickets pre-compose denormalized display strings (metaLine, assignedDetails, requesterContact, requesterDetails, categoryDetails, createdDetails) that duplicate their component fields.

Raw-HTML variables inserted via triple-stache (or double-stache in surveys) that the registry must type as 'raw-html', not 'string': ticket.changes, ticket.resolution, ticket.description (en/pt only), project.changes, comment.content/contentHtml, tenantLinksHtml, credits table rows, and surveys rating_buttons_html/survey_url (which are DOUBLE-stache despite carrying raw HTML - an escaping hazard).

date-string fields are inconsistent about formatting: most are pre-formatted display strings (invoice.dueDate 'August 9, 2026', appointmentDate 'Monday, July 21, 2026'), some are ISO (submittedAt, expires_at, ticket.closedAt), and some are RAW DB values (project.startDate '2026-05-01'). A single 'date-string' type hides three different contracts.

number-typed fields are passed as raw numbers to the email path but stringified for the parallel in-app notification path (opportunities digest.*, credits.daysRemaining). The registry's declared type depends on which channel consumes it.

URL variables carry a portal-vs-MSP duality on the same path: ticket.url, project.url, and credits.url resolve to a client-portal host for external recipients and an /msp host for internal recipients - the path does not encode which. sla ticketUrl is additionally a RELATIVE path, unlike every other absolute URL.

Whole templates have no wired send path, so their variable contracts are template-inferred rather than call-site-verified: invoice-generated, payment-overdue, payment-received (invoices), milestone-completed, task-updated (projects), and the entire time category (time-entry-approved/rejected/submitted, whose rich timeEntry.* object is built by a workflow handler absent from this checkout).

Per-template extras that resist a pure shared-block model: appointments needs stage-specific date pairs (requested* vs appointment*) and cancellation policy fields; sla needs slaType/thresholdPercent/escalationLevel/escalationReason; surveys needs a whole rating sub-block (rating_buttons_html, rating_links_text, rating_scale, rating_type, rating_labels, prompt_text, thank_you_text); opportunities is a single self-contained digest.* block used by exactly one template; tenant-recovery pre-bakes per-org login links into tenantLinksHtml/tenantLinksText rather than exposing an array.

referenceNumber (appointments) is a single template variable with two runtime formats depending on the call site (public route 'APT-...' vs portal 8-char id slice) - the registry example value can only represent one.

The shared _shared/emailLayout.cjs wrapper contributes currentYear + companyName (footer) and headerTitle/headerMeta, but the vast majority of templates override footerText and pass their own headerTitle, so those wrapper variables are present in sample data yet render nowhere - a source of phantom 'available' variables.
risks: Keying the registry by literal handlebars path will FRAGMENT one logical variable into 2-3 entries (ticket.id / ticketNumber / ticket_number; contactEmail / supportEmail / clientLocationEmail; recipientName / requesterName / contactName / userName). Without an explicit alias/canonical-name layer, templates in different categories will reference different registry entries for the same concept and validation cannot cross-check them.

Declaring a variable 'available' at the block level is unsafe: availability is per-template and per-locale. A block-global 'available' flag would wrongly promise values that are assembled-unused, intentionally hidden (client-facing templates), referenced-but-never-supplied (recipientName in project tasks, task.description, pl-only paths), or absent because the template has no send path.

Typing raw-HTML fields as plain 'string' risks the registry driving an escaping change that breaks rendering (ticket.changes, project.changes, comment.contentHtml, rating_buttons_html). Conversely the surveys double-stache-on-raw-HTML case is a latent XSS/mangling bug the registry could either fix or accidentally cement.

The company.name client-vs-MSP collision means a single 'company' block entry cannot be correct for both invoices and billing; conflating them would propagate the existing credit-expiring empty-render bug and could mis-address invoices.

The three date-string contracts (pre-formatted vs ISO vs raw DB) and the number-as-number-vs-string channel split mean a single declared type per variable will be wrong for some consumer; sample/preview data generated from the registry may not match real send-time values.

Templates with no verified send path (invoices generated/overdue/received, projects milestone/task-updated, all of time) give the registry aspirational contracts - any 'available' variables for them are guesses from template markup, and building preview/validation on them asserts a data shape no code produces.

Localized templates (pl, fr) reference variant paths the base call site never assembles. If the registry is derived only from the default-locale template or only from the call site, it will silently omit locale-specific required paths, and completeness checks will pass while pl/fr recipients get empty fields.

Denormalized/derived fields (metaLine, assignedDetails, requesterContact, categoryDetails, createdDetails, credits.totalAmount) are computed upstream, not independent inputs. A registry that treats them as first-class variables invites drift where an author sets the components but not the composed string (or vice versa) with no way to detect the inconsistency.

URL portal-vs-MSP duality and sla's relative-vs-absolute URL mean 'url' is not a self-describing type; a registry example or validation that assumes an absolute app URL will misrepresent sla ticketUrl and the client-portal variants.

Category-specific sub-blocks (survey rating.*, sla clock/escalation fields, appointment stage/policy fields, opportunities digest.*) are used by exactly one or few templates. Forcing them into shared blocks adds unused surface; leaving them out leaves those templates without registry coverage. The registry needs a clear shared-vs-local boundary policy or it will either bloat or under-cover.

referenceNumber's dual runtime format (and similar per-call-site divergences) means the registry cannot capture a single authoritative example/format per variable; downstream consumers relying on a documented format (e.g. parsing 'APT-...') will break on the other call site.
